name: Whole Wheat Oatmeal Bread
directions: Place Ingredients in the order of your breadmaker's instructions into the pan.
Select dough cycle.
Afte dough cycle has completed, set on basic cycle and begin.
I have learned this makes the bread rise better.

name: Honey Wheat Bread
directions: Add wet ingredients to bread pan of bread machine.
Add dry ingredients, ending with flour. Set to dough cycle. Shape dough into loaves and place into two greased bread pans.
Let rise. Bake 375 35 to 40 minute Let cool completely before slicing-if you can wait that long.

name: Perfect Whole Wheat Bread
directions: Combine first 8 ingredients. Mix well.
Add whole wheat flour until dough is sticky but not wet.
Turn out onto oiled surface. Divide into 5 even portions. Put into greased bread pans.
Cover with a cloth and let rise @ 1/2 hour or until a slight rise is visible.
bake in preheated 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.
Turn out baked loaves onto cooling rack and let cool at least 1 hour before slicing or putting into a bag. Freezes well for 1 month if tightly wrapped.

name: Bread
directions: mix and bake.

name: Whole Grain Wheat Bread
directions: In a bowl stir the Whole wheat flour, bran, and 1 1/4 cups of water. Let it sit for 30 minute.
In a bowl or glass measure, stir the warm milk and 1 TBSP white sugar until sugar dissolves, add yeast and stir, let stand until foamy (5 min).
In mixer bowl, mix together the whole wheat mixture, milk mixture, brown sugar , cornmeal, oats, flaxseed, and salt.
Add 2 1/2 cups bread flour, and mix in the mixer with dough hook attatchment 5-7 minute Add a little more flour as needed if the dough is sticky.
Form into a ball by folding sides underneath.  Place in a greased bowl, cover and let rise until doubled in size.
Punch down dough and dump out onto a lightly floured surface.  Fold top edge up and into the center of the dough. Continue with the other three sides until you have formed a rectangle.

Then begin to roll into a log pinching into itself as you go. Pinch the seam, and fold the ends undereath, and place into a greased loaf pan. Brush the top with water and sprinkle with oats and bran. Cover lightly with plastic wrap and let rise until doubled in size.
Bake at 400 degrees for 25-30 minute.

name: 3 Flour Bread
directions: Combine sugar and warm water and then add dry yeast.
Combine flours, salt in a big bowl.
After the yeast has risen, about 10-15 minutes later, mix it with flours and also add olive oil.
Let the dough rise twice.
Divide the dough into 4 mini bread pieces and bake in a 400F oven.

name: Multi Grain Bread
directions: Knead for 8 minutes. Let rise in the oven (turned off) with a pan of hot boiling water on the bottom for ~60-65 minutes. Portion it into two even loaves (I use a scale), shape them to fit a lightly oiled loaf pan. Boil some more water and replace the water in the oven and let the loaves rise for ~45 minutes, then bake at 375F for 30 minutes. If you want a lighter crust, wrap aluminum foil over the loaf pans after about 20 minutes of baking.
Or of course, skip all that and use a bread machine (you might want to halve the recipe though).

name: Honey Wheat Bread
directions: Place honey, yeast and hot water in bowl of mixer fitted with dough hook.  Stir lightly to combine.  Let sit until slightly foamy and yeasty smelling.
Melt butter, let cool to room temperature.
Add the rest of ingredients (including cooled butter) in order given.  May need to adjust the amount of flour or water depending on how dough is coming together.  Mix well at lower speed.
Once dough is nice cohesive mass, turn up speed to knead.
Once fully kneaded, turn out to rise.
If done in Kitchen Aid: let dough rise until double in same bowl.  Then punch down, divide and place into 3 pans.  Set aside to rise.
If done in Bosch: divide dough and place into 3 pans.  Set aside to rise.
Bake the pans in 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.
Immediately remove from pan to wire rack to cool.
Enjoy!

name: Healthy Household Bread
directions: Dissolve honey in water.  Add yeast.  Leave 10 - 15 minutes to bubble.
Sift flours, adding husks left over from sifting into the sifted flour, and salt.  Stir in the LSA and sunflower seeds.
Add yeast mixture and oil.  Knead until texture has developed.  This takes about 4 - 5 minutes in the Kenwood mixer, but would take longer if doing it by hand.
Grease and flour a bread tin.  Place dough into tin and leave to rise.
Bake at 190 degrees C for about 30 - 32 minutes.
